comment_text.snp5.8:2.1: The extraneous variant reading sīlabbatena (not in the Niddesa) appears, oddly, in place of the reciter’s identification of the speaker. The parallel phrase at Snp 4.9:5.1 has it on the next line.

root_text.snp5.8:3.6: Kaccissu te bhagavā tattha yatā carantā,
root_text.snp5.8:3.8: Pucchāmi taṁ bhagavā brūhi me taṁ”.
root_text.snp5.8:5.9: Pucchāmi taṁ bhagavā brūhi me taṁ”.
root_text.snp5.8:6.4: Ye sīdha diṭṭhaṁ va sutaṁ mutaṁ vā,
root_text.snp5.8:7.3: Ye sīdha diṭṭhaṁ va sutaṁ mutaṁ vā,

translation_text.snp5.8:1.1: “People say there are sages in the world,”
translation_text.snp5.8:1.4: Is someone called a sage because of their knowledge,
translation_text.snp5.8:1.5: or because of their way of life?”
translation_text.snp5.8:2.1: “Experts do not speak of a sage in terms of
translation_text.snp5.8:2.3: Those who are sages live far from the crowd, I say,
translation_text.snp5.8:2.4: untroubled, with no need for hope.”
translation_text.snp5.8:3.3: “who speak of purity in terms of what is seen or heard,
translation_text.snp5.8:3.4: or in terms of precepts and vows,
translation_text.snp5.8:3.5: or in terms of countless different things.
translation_text.snp5.8:3.7: have they crossed over rebirth and old age, good fellow?
translation_text.snp5.8:3.8: I ask you, Blessed One; please tell me this.”
translation_text.snp5.8:4.3: “who speak of purity in terms of what is seen or heard,
translation_text.snp5.8:4.4: or in terms of precepts and vows,
translation_text.snp5.8:4.5: or in terms of countless different things.
translation_text.snp5.8:4.6: Even though they live self-controlled in that matter,
translation_text.snp5.8:4.7: they’ve not crossed over rebirth and old age, <j>I declare.”
translation_text.snp5.8:5.3: “who speak of purity in terms of what is seen or heard,
translation_text.snp5.8:5.4: or in terms of precepts and vows,
translation_text.snp5.8:5.5: or in terms of countless different things.
translation_text.snp5.8:5.6: You say they have not crossed the flood, sage.
translation_text.snp5.8:5.7: So who then in the world of gods and humans
translation_text.snp5.8:5.8: has crossed over rebirth and old age, good fellow?
translation_text.snp5.8:5.9: I ask you, Blessed One; please tell me this.”
translation_text.snp5.8:6.1: “I don’t say that all ascetics and brahmins,”
translation_text.snp5.8:6.3: “are shrouded by rebirth and old age.
translation_text.snp5.8:6.4: There are those here who have given up all
translation_text.snp5.8:6.5: that is seen, heard, and thought, <j>and precepts and vows,
translation_text.snp5.8:6.6: who have given up all the countless different things.
translation_text.snp5.8:6.7: Fully understanding craving, they are free of defilements,
translation_text.snp5.8:6.8: those people, I say, have crossed the flood.”
translation_text.snp5.8:7.1: “I rejoice in the words of the great seer!
translation_text.snp5.8:7.2: You have expounded non-attachment well, Gotama.
translation_text.snp5.8:7.3: There are those here who have given up all
translation_text.snp5.8:7.4: that is seen, heard, and thought, <j>and precepts and vows,
translation_text.snp5.8:7.5: who have given up all the countless different things.
translation_text.snp5.8:7.6: Fully understanding craving, they are free of defilements,
translation_text.snp5.8:7.7: those people, I agree, have crossed the flood.”

variant_text.snp5.8:3.3: Diṭṭhassutenāpi → diṭṭhena sutenāpi (bj); diṭṭhe sutenāpi (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1, mr)
variant_text.snp5.8:5.6: muni → sace muni (si, pts-vp-pli1); te ce munī (cnd5:439 [Pucchāniddesa/ Nandamāṇavapucchāniddesa])
